title: crabgent architecture atlas
tagline: A functional map of the runtime, with crates as implementation evidence.
principles: Kernel owns the run loop. Transport stays outside.
Policy gates actions before side effects.
Hooks compose behavior without becoming a second kernel.
Crates implement capabilities; capabilities are the navigation surface.

maps.title: Runtime Spine
maps.subtitle: Follow one user turn from channel ingress to LLM, tools, memory, outbound delivery, and lifecycle signals.
maps.legend: Solid lines are runtime flow. Amber lines are policy or trust boundaries. Green lines are persistence and recall.

nodes.label: Ingress
nodes.summary: Slack, Matrix, Telegram, MCP, or direct callers turn outside events into typed crabgent inputs.

nodes.label: Subject + Session
nodes.summary: Subject carries auth identity, Owner carries persistence identity, and Session gives a conversation-shaped history.

nodes.label: Policy Gate
nodes.summary: Every meaningful side-effect crosses Action and PolicyHook before it runs.

nodes.label: Hook Chain
nodes.summary: FIFO hooks enrich, observe, replace, deny, compact, inject, log, or cancel without becoming transport code.

nodes.label: Provider + Model
nodes.summary: Provider-neutral requests are reshaped into Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, STT, image-generation, or embedding calls.

nodes.label: Tool Loop
nodes.summary: Tool calls are parsed, policy-gated, executed with ToolCtx, observed by hooks, and optionally compacted or cached.

nodes.label: Memory + Recall
nodes.summary: Memory classes, recall strategies, embedding-aware search, lifecycle tools, and consolidation turn history into useful context.

nodes.label: Calendar / Cron / Task
nodes.summary: Time hints, scheduled jobs, and background tasks create controlled work beyond one immediate LLM response.

nodes.label: Outbound + Progress
nodes.summary: Replies, channel side-effects, notifications, reactions, typing, and reasoning progress leave the kernel through typed sinks.

nodes.label: Stores
nodes.summary: In-memory, SQLite, and Postgres backends persist sessions, memory, cron, and tool-cache state behind traits.

nodes.label: Vision + Audio
nodes.summary: Channel-side media validation keeps bytes typed before providers reshape them for model-specific wire formats.

nodes.label: MCP Bridge
nodes.summary: MCP client imports remote tools; MCP server exports crabgent tools and chat through JSON-RPC sessions.

nodes.label: Cancel + Shutdown
nodes.summary: Stop-patterns, hook cancellation, external cancellation, task drains, and graceful shutdown converge on explicit cancellation roots.

maps.title: Channel Boundary
maps.subtitle: Adapters normalize outside conversations into safe kernel runs, then route typed outbound effects back to the right transport.
maps.legend: Ingress hardening happens before kernel context is built. Outbound is tool-driven and policy-gated.

nodes.label: Slack
nodes.summary: Socket Mode plus Web API: threads, uploads, reactions, reads, progress.

nodes.label: Matrix
nodes.summary: Plaintext sync, room threading, reactions, edit/delete/upload/read.

nodes.label: Telegram
nodes.summary: Long polling, direct conversations, voice/audio, reactions, HTML output hint.

nodes.label: InboundEvent
nodes.summary: Channel-agnostic body, participants, channel kind, thread root, timestamps, and attrs.

nodes.label: Sanitize + Persona Boundary
nodes.summary: Inbound text is length-capped, sanitized, and framed before it becomes LLM-facing content.

nodes.label: KernelChannelInbox
nodes.summary: Builds RunRequest, resolves session, applies stop patterns, dispatches into Kernel.

nodes.label: ChannelSink + Router
nodes.summary: Routes send, read, react, edit, delete, upload, notify_user to registered channel implementations.

nodes.label: Channel Tools
nodes.summary: LLM-facing channel tools create outbound effects under policy.

nodes.label: Typing + Progress
nodes.summary: Transport-specific UX signals consume reasoning and tool-progress events without changing the run result.

maps.title: Context, Identity, Session
maps.subtitle: Auth identity, persistence identity, conversation state, and model overrides stay separate until a RunRequest is built.
maps.legend: Identity split is central: Subject is not Owner, and MemoryScope is the scope vector.

nodes.label: Subject
nodes.summary: Auth-facing actor with attrs used by policy and adapters.

nodes.label: Owner
nodes.summary: Persistence identity for sessions, memory, cron, and scoped data.

nodes.label: MemoryScope
nodes.summary: Single reusable scope vector across memory and cron filtering.

nodes.label: SessionManager
nodes.summary: Finds or creates the conversation session and prepends durable history.

nodes.label: SessionPersistHook
nodes.summary: Persists canonical run messages, including compacted views and channel outbound fan-out.

nodes.label: Model Override
nodes.summary: Session and global model overrides are stored and validated through the model tool surface.

nodes.label: RunRequest
nodes.summary: Kernel-facing request carries messages, model, subject, cancel handles, and runtime metadata.

maps.title: Policy And Hook Mesh
maps.subtitle: Policy decides whether a side effect may happen. Hooks enrich and observe the run while respecting explicit cancellation.
maps.legend: Deny is terminal. Replace mutates the typed value. Continue keeps the chain moving.

nodes.label: Action
nodes.summary: Typed description of the side effect or query being requested.

nodes.label: PolicyHook
nodes.summary: Fail-closed policy surface. KernelBuilder requires one before build.

nodes.label: HookChain
nodes.summary: FIFO composition over before_llm, after_llm, tool, event, and shutdown hook points.

nodes.label: TimeHint
nodes.summary: Adds current time, weekday, timezone, and holiday hints to system prompt.

nodes.label: Compact
nodes.summary: Token-aware semantic compaction with continuity, archive-before-compact, and per-run serialization.

nodes.label: Inject
nodes.summary: Runtime message injection keyed by RunId.

nodes.label: Log + Thinking
nodes.summary: Events and reasoning deltas become logging and user-visible progress signals.

nodes.label: Cancel Root
nodes.summary: Hooks, stop patterns, or external callers cancel through RunCtx and carry CancelReason.

nodes.label: Kernel Loop
nodes.summary: The only place that turns policy, hooks, provider calls, and tool loops into a run result.

maps.title: Provider, Model, Media
maps.subtitle: The kernel speaks provider-neutral types. Provider crates own model discovery, wire shape, auth, retries, and model-specific media reshaping.
maps.legend: Model capabilities are the guardrail between a RunRequest and a provider wire body.

nodes.label: ModelRegistry
nodes.summary: Catalog of ModelInfo and ModelCapabilities used for validation and overrides.

nodes.label: LlmRequest
nodes.summary: Provider-neutral message, model, tool, image/audio, and reasoning request shape.

nodes.label: Provider Trait
nodes.summary: Common complete, stream, capabilities, and models surface.

nodes.label: Transport Helpers
nodes.summary: Cancellation-aware HTTP retries, auth classification, capped body reads.

nodes.label: Anthropic
nodes.summary: Streaming, prompt caching, model discovery, and Anthropic wire format.

nodes.label: OpenAI
nodes.summary: Chat Completions, Responses/Codex OAuth, prompt caching, STT.

nodes.label: Google
nodes.summary: Gemini discovery, generateContent, image-generation support.

nodes.label: STT + Embedding
nodes.summary: Speech-to-text and local embedding provider traits extend the same provider-neutral pattern.

maps.title: Tool Execution Mesh
maps.subtitle: LLM tool calls become typed args, policy-gated actions, tool results, cached output, and sometimes channel or persistence effects.
maps.legend: Every tool has Args + Op + TOOL_NAME + schema + execute. ToolCtx carries trusted runtime context.

nodes.label: ToolCall
nodes.summary: Provider-originated tool name and JSON args.

nodes.label: Args + Op
nodes.summary: Serde parses one operation enum and validates required fields.

nodes.label: ToolCtx
nodes.summary: Subject, session, tool roots, current model context, and policy-visible metadata.

nodes.label: Action Gate
nodes.summary: Tool-specific Action goes through PolicyHook before execution.

nodes.label: ToolResult
nodes.summary: Result can be normal, error-shaped, cache reference, or channel outbound signal.

nodes.label: Memory Tools
nodes.summary: Search, store, get, delete, archive, unarchive, expiry, forget.

nodes.label: Calendar / Cron / Task
nodes.summary: Date operations, cron CRUD, background task create/list/get/cancel.

nodes.label: Model Tool
nodes.summary: List, get, current, set/clear session override, set/clear global override.

nodes.label: Channel Tools
nodes.summary: Send, read, react, edit, delete, upload, notify_user.

nodes.label: Tool Cache
nodes.summary: Large tool output is replaced with cache pointers and read back by cache_read.

maps.title: Memory Lifecycle
maps.subtitle: Memory is not one bag of strings. Class, scope, recall strategy, embedding, lifecycle, consolidation, and store backend all stay explicit.
maps.legend: Semantic category is MemoryClass. Conversation kind is not class.

nodes.label: MemoryClass
nodes.summary: Semantic, episodic, recency, notes, user_profile, skill, tools.

nodes.label: MemoryScope
nodes.summary: Owner, channel, thread scope reused across memory and cron.

nodes.label: Recall Strategy
nodes.summary: Blended FTS, importance, recency decay, and embeddings produce ranked recall.

nodes.label: Embeddings
nodes.summary: Optional provider-backed vectors augment FTS in tool and store paths.

nodes.label: Memory Tool
nodes.summary: LLM-facing search/store/get/delete/archive/unarchive/extend/forget lifecycle.

nodes.label: MemoryStore
nodes.summary: In-memory, SQLite, and Postgres implement the same memory persistence contract.

nodes.label: Consolidation
nodes.summary: Extraction, deduplication, conflict resolution, stale cleanup, audit, checkpoint.

nodes.label: Persist Hook
nodes.summary: Optional hook turns run messages into memory records under policy and scope.

maps.title: Time, Jobs, Tasks
maps.subtitle: Temporal context and deferred execution are explicit surfaces, not hidden loops inside the kernel.
maps.legend: Calendar answers time questions. Cron schedules prompts. Task runs controlled background work.

nodes.label: TimeHintHook
nodes.summary: Injects now, weekday, timezone, holiday, and authoring-time guidance.

nodes.label: Calendar Tool
nodes.summary: Holiday list/next/check, days_between, date arithmetic, weekday info.

nodes.label: Cron Tool
nodes.summary: LLM-facing create/list/get/update/delete/pause/resume schedule surface.

nodes.label: CronScheduler
nodes.summary: Claims due jobs, runs prompts through executor, drains on shutdown.

nodes.label: Task Tool
nodes.summary: Create, list, get, cancel with context injection and optional blocking wait.

nodes.label: TaskExecutor
nodes.summary: Depth cap, parallel cap, cancellation propagation, parent_task_id propagation.

nodes.label: Scoped Store
nodes.summary: Cron and task metadata persist behind stores using Owner/MemoryScope vocabulary.

maps.title: MCP Bridge
maps.subtitle: crabgent can consume remote MCP tools and expose its own tools plus chat through the MCP server surface.
maps.legend: Client and server are separate boundaries around the same tool/kernel concepts.

nodes.label: Remote MCP Server
nodes.summary: External Streamable HTTP/SSE server with its own tool catalog.

nodes.label: MCP Client
nodes.summary: Discovers tools, prefixes names, wraps remote calls as crabgent tools.

nodes.label: Kernel Tools
nodes.summary: Registered Tool trait objects exposed to providers and optionally MCP server.

nodes.label: JSON-RPC Dispatch
nodes.summary: Bearer-authenticated MCP server request handling and SSE helpers.

nodes.label: MCP Sessions
nodes.summary: UUIDv7 session registry, bounded LRU, per-session cancellation token.

nodes.label: Native Chat Tool
nodes.summary: MCP chat dispatch builds a RunRequest and calls the kernel with session cancellation.

nodes.label: Kernel
nodes.summary: Shared execution core for chat and tool calls.

maps.title: Vision And Audio
maps.subtitle: Bytes become typed payloads at channel boundaries. Providers do model-specific wire reshaping without revalidating trust-boundary decisions.
maps.legend: Validation belongs near ingress. Base64 and provider-specific layout are wire details.

nodes.label: Channel Files
nodes.summary: Adapters receive files, images, voice, and audio from outside transports.

nodes.label: Validate + Cache
nodes.summary: MIME, size, magic-byte, and filesystem storage happen before kernel/provider handoff.

nodes.label: Typed Payloads
nodes.summary: ImagePayload, AudioPayload, and FilePayload travel as typed content blocks.

nodes.label: Capability Preflight
nodes.summary: Kernel checks model capability before sending vision/audio to a provider.

nodes.label: Provider Reshape
nodes.summary: Providers map typed payloads into wire-specific multipart, JSON, or content arrays.

nodes.label: Speech To Text
nodes.summary: STT inbox and providers turn audio into transcript-prefixed text.

nodes.label: LLM Request
nodes.summary: Final model request receives typed media or transcript text depending on capability and path.

maps.title: Persistence Backplane
maps.subtitle: Storage is a set of trait contracts. Runtime code depends on stores, not on a specific database.
maps.legend: In-memory is the default. SQLite is local durable. Postgres is opt-in and heavier.

nodes.label: Store Traits
nodes.summary: SessionStore, MemoryStore, CronStore, ToolCacheStore and record types.

nodes.label: Memory Records
nodes.summary: FTS, importance, recency, embedding, archive, expiry.

nodes.label: Session Records
nodes.summary: Messages, thread/session ids, model override, search.

nodes.label: Cron + Cache
nodes.summary: Scoped cron jobs and tool-output cache records.

nodes.label: In-Memory
nodes.summary: Fast default backend for tests and local simple deployments.

nodes.label: SQLite
nodes.summary: Single-node durable backend with embedded migrations.

nodes.label: Postgres
nodes.summary: Opt-in backend with native types, migrations, tsvector FTS and vector search.

nodes.label: Runtime Users
nodes.summary: Session manager, memory tools, cron scheduler, cache hook consume the trait contracts.

maps.title: Cancellation And Shutdown
maps.subtitle: crabgent separates run cancellation, stop-pattern attribution, task drains, and kernel shutdown instead of hiding lifecycle in ad hoc booleans.
maps.legend: Cancellation has a root and a reason. Shutdown has a grace period and drain semantics.

nodes.label: External Token
nodes.summary: Caller-supplied cancellation token for run or stream.

nodes.label: Stop Pattern
nodes.summary: Channel messages like stop/cancel map to explicit CancelReason.

nodes.label: RunCtx Cancel
nodes.summary: Shared cancellation cell plus OnceLock reason visible to hooks and observers.

nodes.label: Kernel Loop
nodes.summary: Stops cooperatively and returns typed cancellation or shutdown errors.

nodes.label: Task / Cron Drains
nodes.summary: Background executors and schedulers obey cancellation and shutdown grace.

nodes.label: Inbox Shutdown
nodes.summary: ChannelInbox shutdown forwards grace through decorators to lifecycle-aware inboxes.

nodes.label: Shutdown Hooks
nodes.summary: Hook notifications distinguish normal drain, cancelled task, and panic during shutdown.
